blocks.body: Factoring by grouping is used for polynomials with 4 terms. You group terms in pairs and factor out the GCF from each pair, then factor out the common binomial.
Steps: (1) Group terms into two pairs. (2) Factor GCF from each pair. (3) If the remaining binomials match, factor out that binomial. Example: ax + ay + bx + by = a(x+y) + b(x+y) = (a+b)(x+y).
If the two groups do not produce the same binomial, try rearranging the original terms in a different order, then group again.
